headline: Intuit announced the results of its Annual Meeting of Stockholders, including the election of 11 directors, ratification of auditors, and approval of an amended Non-Employee Director Compensation Program.
investor_impact: This is a routine corporate governance filing detailing standard annual meeting outcomes. The predictable re-election of the board, approval of executive compensation, and rejection of the shareholder proposal present no material changes to the company's strategic direction or investment thesis.
key_figures: 11 directors elected to the Board, including CEO Sasan K. Goodarzi and founder Scott D. Cook
Ernst & Young LLP ratified as independent auditor for the fiscal year ending July 31, 2026
228,853,804 votes against the stockholder proposal requesting a report on diversity and inclusion program ROI
215,761,247 votes for the advisory approval of executive compensation
